title: Conditional Sentences
definition: Conditional sentences describe a condition (if-clause) and its result (main clause). English has four main types, called Zero, First, Second, and Third conditional.
rules: Zero Conditional (general truths): If + present simple, present simple. Example: If you heat ice, it melts.
First Conditional (real future possibility): If + present simple, will + V1. Example: If it rains, I will stay home.
Second Conditional (unreal/hypothetical present): If + past simple, would + V1. Example: If I won the lottery, I would travel the world.
Third Conditional (unreal past - regret): If + past perfect, would have + V3. Example: If I had studied, I would have passed.
'If' can be replaced with 'unless' (= if not): Unless you hurry, you will miss the bus.
examples: Zero: If you don't water plants, they die.
First: If I finish work early, I will call you.
First: If it rains tomorrow, we will cancel the picnic.
Second: If I were rich, I would buy a big house.
Second: If she studied harder, she would get better grades.
Third: If I had known, I would have helped you.
Third: If they had left earlier, they wouldn't have missed the flight.

commonMistakes.wrong: If I will see him, I will tell him.
commonMistakes.right: If I see him, I will tell him.
commonMistakes.why: Don't use 'will' in the if-clause of the First Conditional; use present simple.

commonMistakes.wrong: If I was you, I would apologize.
commonMistakes.right: If I were you, I would apologize.
commonMistakes.why: In the Second Conditional, use 'were' for all subjects (I/he/she/it) in formal English.

commonMistakes.wrong: If I would have known, I would have come.
commonMistakes.right: If I had known, I would have come.
commonMistakes.why: The if-clause of the Third Conditional uses past perfect, not 'would have'.

quiz.question: Choose the correct First Conditional sentence:
options: If it rains, I will stay home.
If it will rain, I stay home.
If it rain, I will stay home.
If it rains, I stayed home.
quiz.answer: If it rains, I will stay home.
quiz.explanation: First Conditional: If + present simple, will + V1.

quiz.question: Choose the correct Third Conditional sentence:
options: If I had studied, I would pass.
If I studied, I would have passed.
If I had studied, I would have passed.
If I have studied, I would have passed.
quiz.answer: If I had studied, I would have passed.
quiz.explanation: Third Conditional: If + past perfect, would have + V3.

quiz.question: If I ___ (be) you, I would apologize. (Second Conditional)
quiz.explanation: Second Conditional uses 'were' for all subjects.

quiz.sentence: If I will have time, I will visit you.
quiz.correction: If I have time, I will visit you.
quiz.explanation: Don't use 'will' in the if-clause of the First Conditional.
